name: sandbox_runner
description: Execute Python code in a fully isolated temporary virtual environment. Creates a fresh venv in a temp directory, optionally installs pip packages, writes and runs the script, captures stdout/stderr, then destroys the entire environment. Use for: data generation, file manipulation, one-off computations, library-dependent tasks. The sandbox is ephemeral — nothing persists after the call. For pure computation with no package needs, prefer execute_python (faster, no venv overhead).

code.description: Python source code to execute inside the sandbox.

packages.description: pip packages to install before running (e.g. ['numpy', 'pandas==2.0']). Each string is passed directly to pip install. Optional.

timeout.description: Maximum execution time in seconds for the script itself. Package installation has a separate 120s limit. Default: 60.

input_data.description: Optional string passed as stdin to the script.

session_id.description: Session ID for log_manager integration. Auto-injected by the engine.
